21.reason: [The problem requires reading from "article.txt" in the current directory, not an absolute path like "D:/article.txt". This will cause file not found on systems without this exact path.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [The problem requires reading from "article.txt" in the current directory, not an absolute path like "D:/article.txt". This will cause file not found on systems without this exact path.]

27.reason: [The loop starts from r->link (i.e., skips the first node). If head is not NULL, the first word is never printed. It should start from r = head.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [The loop starts from r->link (i.e., skips the first node). If head is not NULL, the first word is never printed. It should start from r = head.]

37.reason: [This condition is redundant and slightly incorrect: tolower(letter) is always lowercase, but the condition should just use isalpha() as in reference. Also, it fails to handle non-English letters properly, though problem says English text. More critically, after EOF, if a word was being read (i>0), it's not returned.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [This condition is redundant and slightly incorrect: tolower(letter) is always lowercase, but the condition should just use isalpha() as in reference. Also, it fails to handle non-English letters properly, though problem says English text. More critically, after EOF, if a word was being read (i>0), it's not returned.]

41.reason: [This condition is wrong: getin is always non-NULL (it's an array passed by caller). It should check if i > 0 to terminate a word. As written, it breaks even when no word has been read (i==0), returning empty string. Also, function doesn't return EOF correctly when end of file is reached after a word.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [This condition is wrong: getin is always non-NULL (it's an array passed by caller). It should check if i > 0 to terminate a word. As written, it breaks even when no word has been read (i==0), returning empty string. Also, function doesn't return EOF correctly when end of file is reached after a word.]

46.reason: [Missing return statement: function must return EOF when end-of-file is reached and no word remains, or 1 (or non-EOF) when a word is read. As written, it returns undefined value.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [Missing return statement: function must return EOF when end-of-file is reached and no word remains, or 1 (or non-EOF) when a word is read. As written, it returns undefined value.]
